definition: Portfolio construction should shift 10-15% from nominal duration toward convexity-based hedging including trend-following (CTA), long volatility, and real assets (TIPS, gold, commodity producers). Under positive/unstable correlation, only strategies with positive convexity in both tails provide genuine diversification.
evidence: TIPS at 2.3-2.5% real yield provide inflation protection
Contrarian TLT call overlay exploits elevated put skew
Ordinal ranking (short-duration > long-duration equities, real > nominal bonds) is high conviction
10-15% reallocation magnitude is 'a judgment call, not a derived optimum'
open_questions: Execution details add 200-300bp uncertainty to Sharpe estimates
Whether CTA strategies remain effective if correlation oscillation becomes the norm
